post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 318 (Newbie Game 121): Mafia Around the World

Important Rules:
Do not edit or delete posts
Do not post during night
Do not post if you are dead or if you are not in the game
Do not talk about anything related to this game outside this thread
Read all the rules!


Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red, blue, green, or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Confirm your participation in the game by making a post with your favourite country to travel to.
  • Voting/Elimination Procedures
    • 2.1: Post votes in code tags in the format 
      [code]Vote: <insert player here>[/‎code]

      Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. A proper vote looks like 
      Vote: fdas
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang/elimination to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post 
      Vote: No Hang
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote. Unvote in the format 
      Vote: Unvote

      An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no player has been hanged, the amount of votes required for a Hang or a No Hang will be reduced by 1. The vote threshold will reduce by 1 vote every 24 hours after this. For example, if there are 8 players alive in the game, 5 votes will be required to hang someone and 4 votes are required for a no hang. Once the deadline passes, 4 votes will be sufficient to hang someone and 3 votes are sufficient for a no hang. 24 hours after the deadline, 3 votes will be sufficient to hang someone and 2 votes are sufficient for a no hang.
    • 2.10: If the vote threshold is decreased and two or more options have both reached the threshold required, then the game will enter tiebreak. During tiebreak, votes can continue to be changed. If at any point in time, only one option has enough votes to meet the threshold required for the option to be enacted, day will immediately end. Or, if any option gains votes and now exceeds the vote threshold by an amount of votes strictly greater than the amount of votes any other option exceeds their vote threshold by, that option will win and day will immediately end. If 24 hours have passed since tiebreak has begun, and the votes are still deadlocked, there will be no hang.
    • 2.11: After an option has won the vote, it will become twilight and players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will 1 week long. Each night will be 1 day long. The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.3: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 24-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 3 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in 
      code
      in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I (RTG) am the moderator of Mafia Game 318
    • 5.2: Blue-colored text is used for the moderator posts including votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.3: Red-colored text is used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 24 hours, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.6: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules. Again, if unsure of a rule, clarify with the moderator.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game will use the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons


Role PMs
Vanilla Townie wrote:
You are a Citizen of the United States, also known as a Vanilla Townie.

You have always supported the war on organized crime. Standing behind your country, you believe the threats to the well being of all people of the world are the crime syndicates that bring drugs, death, and destruction to the average human being. Therefore, you want to do all you can to eliminate these pests from the world. Your activist voice and vote is what gives you the power to end organized crime.

You have the power of your voice and your vote. You win when all of the Mafia have been eliminated.
Town Cop wrote:
You are a Member of MI6, also known as a Town Cop.

You are a specialized entity specifically designed for intel on enemy forces that may counter your ultimate goal. You have been able to infiltrate criminal organizations in the past and sus out members, so what will make this task of yours any different?

Each night you may investigate one player. You will receive Town, Mafia, or No Result. You also have the power of your voice and your vote. You win when all of the Mafia have been eliminated.
Town Doctor wrote:
You are a Doctor for WHO, also known as a Town Doctor.

You are involved with protecting the world from widespread diseases. However, you have a secret mission to also protect the broad spectrum of people around the world from organized crime. You accomplish this through you're advanced on site medical training for all wounds no matter the complexity.

Each night, you may target a player to protect from a NK. Assuming you are not roleblocked, that player will be protected from being NKed that night. You may not target the same player two nights in a row. You may protect yourself. You also have the power of your voice and your vote. You win when all of the Mafia have been eliminated.
Town Tracker wrote:
You are a Member of the DEA, also known as a Town Tracker.

You are based in Guadalajara on a mission to reveal the drug traffickers that are trying to infect the world with illicit material. However, as an American entity, you have no authority to act in Mexico, only observe. Therefore, you are tasked with watching all the happenings in the city to see if anything is out of place and report it to the higher ups in DC.

Each Night, you may target a player to track. At the end of the night you will be informed who that player targeted if any. You also have the power of your voice and your vote. You win when all of the Mafia have been eliminated.
Town Jailkeeper wrote:
You are a Guard of the ICC Detention Centre, also known as a Town Jailkeeper.

You are tasked with making sure the suspects make it to trial. Whether they are guilty or not, you don't care. You just want to make sure they survive by the word of law. You also want to make sure they aren't able to perform any nefarious acts from behind bars either. You watch them closely to make sure there isn't a hair out of place.

Each Night, you may target a player. Assuming you are not roleblocked, that player will be protected from NKs and all their actions will also be blocked that night. You also have the power of your voice and your vote. You win when all of the Mafia have been eliminated.
1-Shot Bulletproof Townie wrote:
You are an Armoured Peacekeeper, also known as a 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie.

You live on the front lines in the name of world order. You have seen many war crimes and many peace deals. You have been a vital part in the war on terror. As a grizzled veteran, you know a thing or two about how to survive and protect yourself from harm's way.

You are protected from the first NK that targets you. You also have the power of your voice and your vote. You win when all of the Mafia have been eliminated.
Mafia Goon wrote:
You are a Member of the Mexican Cartel, also known as a Mafia Goon.

You are all about making money. No matter what you need to do, you just want to have all of life's riches. However, many people around the world seem to be getting in your way. You must get rid of them. You must survive. Therefore, you must maneuver your way around the obstacles that these citizens might throw at you.

Your partner is [insert username]. You may discuss with your partner [url=]here[/url]. Each night, you and your partner may decide which one of you will target a member of town in order to NK them. You also have the power of your voice and your vote. You win when Town is no longer able to eliminate you.
Mafia Roleblocker wrote:
You are a Member of the Sicilian Mafia, also known as a Mafia Roleblocker.

You are all about control. Controlling the Island. Controlling the economy. Controlling the country. You must control it all. However, these international forces are getting in your way. You must take them out. You must conquer so that you can survive and rule all of Italy.

Your partner is [insert username]. You may discuss with your partner [url=]here[/url]. Each night, you may target one player in order to roleblock them. You and your partner may also decide which one of you will target a member of town in order to NK them. You cannot roleblock and perform the NK on the same night. You also have the power of your voice and your vote. You win when Town is no longer able to eliminate you.


Alive (4)
  1. galaxy1220 +EulerNTM, Member of the Mexican Cartel (Mafia Goon)
  2. hockeyfox, Armoured Peacekeeper (1-Shot Bulletproof Townie)
  3. sianlee622 DittoDino K124659, Member of the Mexican Cartel (Mafia Goon)
  4. longhairrabbit the_count_of_monte_cristo, Citizen of the United States (Vanilla Townie)


Dead (5)
strife.fits was hung Day 1. She was a Citizen of the United States, also known as a Vanilla Townie.
sweetbird108 NoSignOfTheta died Night 1. He was a Citizen of the United States, also known as a Vanilla Townie.
Lindseymathlover Unicode_Master03B8 was hung Day 2. He was a Member of the DEA, also known as a Town Tracker.
+mathjay died Night 2. He was a Citizen of the United States, also known as a Vanilla Townie.
++fdas was hung Day 3. He was a Citizen of the United States, also known as a Vanilla Townie.


Events
#48 with Confirm Count 1.1, Activity Check 1.1, and Votecount 1.1
#69 with Confirm Count 1.2, Activity Check 1.2, and Votecount 1.2
#74 with Confirm Count 1.3, Activity Check 1.3, and Votecount 1.3
#93 replacing galaxy1220, Lindseymathlover, and sianlee622
#352 with Confirm Count 1.4, Activity Check 1.4, and Votecount 1.4
#438 sweetbird108 receives 1 warning
#719 with Confirm Count 1.5, Activity Check 1.5, and Votecount 1.5
#853 with Activity Check 1.6 and Votecount 1.6
#923 replacing sweetbird108
#962 with Activity Check 1.7 and Votecount 1.7
#1025 with End of Day 1
#1026 with Start of Day 2
#1050 with Activity Check 2.1 and Votecount 2.1
#1074 with Activity Check 2.2 and Votecount 2.2
#1077 replacing DittoDino
#1127 replacing longhairrabbit
#1128 with Activity Check 2.3 and Votecount 2.3
#1224 with End of Day 2
#1225 with Start of Day 3
#1325 with the End of the Game


PM Prods
fdas - 1


Warnings
 


Votecount 1.0: Start of Day 1

Not Voting (9): fdas, mathjay, galaxy1220, hockeyfox, sweetbird108, strife.fits, sianlee622, longhairrabbit, Lindseymathlover

With 9 alive it takes 5 to hang.

The Game Is Over. Congratulations to the Mafia for Winning the Game.

made by the GOAT ad333:
ad333 wrote:
Ok hi i made a wallpost for the newbies this morning because i was bored & also it's fun I'm sure this will be the one that teaches everybody everything

So Mafia was invented in 1987 by Russian professor D--
(just kidding)

Firstly, hi welcome nice to meet you all!! I'm ad333 & I'm one of the three experienced players in this newbie game, along with WisteriaV and ostriches88. We're here to help you learn how to play the game, so if you have any questions please direct them at us. This includes any terms we use that you're unfamiliar with (I'm particularly guilty of this). But for a start, there's this post!

Second, if you are not a Vanilla Townie, for the purposes of your posts here, yes you are. The following sections are obviously most optimized for players who are not a Vanilla Townie but I'd advise you give them a quick read just if you are so that you're up to date with the strategies. Also I know everybody read all the rules, including the setup but Here's how the setup works
There are 6 possible setups, and one of them is randomly chosen. Nobody knows which one was chosen, but some players have a clue. If the mafia have 2 goons, they know there is either a cop, a jailkeeper, or a tracker and 1-shot bulletproof townie, and similar if they have a roleblocker instead. And each town power role appears in 2 setups, so they also have a clue. The mystery of which setup we're in will change how mafia plays because they don't know what roles there are and have to counterplay all or none of them.
Otherwise if you're a Power Role
(Jailkeeper, Cop, Doctor, Tracker, 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie), mafia will either kiII, roleblock, or neither (if you're 1-Shot Bulletproof) you. The times when you should reveal your role are 1) If you are about to be voted out 2) If you have information that means somebody is mafia. 3) If you have information that means somebody else who is about to be voted out is town. 4) If the game reaches day 3/day 4. Hopefully one of us will be alive to explain why, but briefly this is the stage of the game where nights are scarce and the extra information is helpful. Now for specific Power Role strategies
First of all you can do something different depending on context or if you really disagree, but try to keep these in mind

1. Jailkeeper This is going to be up to your discretion largely, because you both roleblock and protect somebody. When both mafia are alive, you have less chance of targeting the specific mafia member who used the action. This also means if both of them are alive and you target somebody, but somebody dies at night this doesn't confirm them as town.
2. Cop: Again there's a number of options, but you should be trying to target people who are likely to survive to the end. If somebody is acting like a member of the mafia and will probably die the next day, it's probably not the greatest idea to check them. If they are a member of the town, you will have to claim your role and will (in most cases)not be able to use your role again.

You should also find a way to hide your results in your posts, so that in case you die at night without having claimed, we can use the fact that you are a cop to figure out who you checked during the night. This is probably easiest to do by just moving the player up in your reads (if they're town, if they're mafia you should probably just tell everybody that you are the cop and that player is mafia).

3. Doctor: Target whoever you think is going to die. This is normally either a player who is experienced (so that they stop posting in the thread and don't have the chance to figure out who the mafia team is), a player who is towny (because they won't ever get hanged), or a Power Role (normally only one that has revealed themselves). Since mafia normally don't even know if there is a doctor you shouldn't worry too much about trying to outsmart them.

4. Tracker: Ok I'm not going to lie this is one of the weaker roles, but it still can do great things. The first thing to know is the difference between a "No result" and "no visit" answer. The first one means you were roleblocked, the second means your target didn't visit anybody.

If you do get a result that a player visited someone, if they are the player who died, that is the mafia. If not, that's the doctor. If you get a "no visit" result, they could be the mafia whose partner did the kiII instead. This means you should try to target the player who is most likely to be mafia doing a nightkiII. This is subject to the mafia trying to outsmart you so be careful. Once only one mafia is alive you become a lot more powerful, congratulations! You are now basically a cop

5. 1-Shot BulletProof townie. Some people consider this a boring role, I don't. Your goal is to get mafia to try to kiII you, normally by being very towny, or just by surviving until mafia have fewer choices. That is pretty much it.
If you're mafia
First off, we are enemies, but it's my job to help you too (unless the mafia is exactly WisteriaV/ostriches88 in which case I hate you both). It's it's not very fun for anybody if you tell everyone else that you are mafia. That's about it. KiII the players who
1) Are most likely to successfully vote you out
2) Are least likely to get voted out
and/or
3) Have claimed a Power Role
As for how to post... You'll normally have to figure that out but try to post like you are town. As a mafia you have TMI, more on this later, but it makes it hard for you to accurately pretend to be mafia.

Also it might be worth figuring out what to do if your partner is under pressure. Do you try to save them (<- preferred) or vote them out as well to seem extra towny.
Ok I know I just spent a lot of time talking about power Roles but really, these shouldn't matter until at least day 2 and it's best not to talk about them because that's a easy way for mafia to figure out what your role is.

Third, let's talk about the structure of the game. I reccomend you read this just to clear up any questions about this sort of stuff Game structure
There are 7 town members and 2 mafia members. I'll do my best to call them mafia throughout this post & the game but mafia are also known as wolves or scum. The mafia want to kiII everyone and the town want to stop that by voting out the mafia. The town's strength is their numbers, the mafia's strength is knowing each other (and their night kiII).

The game alternates between day and night phases, starting with day 1 right now. During the day, players make posts, and then based on those posts they vote somebody out. It's important not to edit or delete posts or to talk about the game even to people not in the game because it can create information imbalances that shouldn't be there. Some examples
In more malicious cases a player can make a post while one other player is in the thread, claiming a role, for example, and then edit it once they've seen it. This is bad and is as such against the rules. Even if you talk to someone outside of the game, there can be issues such as that player talking to somebody else in the game, or them giving you ideas. Note that this is not an invitation to break the rules or to challenge them (in thread, you can go bug fdas if you want); it's just the first things that come to mind as examples for why the exist. Just don't.
Make a new post in the thread if you want to edit, and if you want to talk about the game outside of the thread... either talk in thread or in your Role PM, or wait for the game to end. The wolves have a private chat where they can talk to each other during the day and the night.

During the night, nobody can post in the thread. The mafia choose somebody to kiII, and the power roles choose somebody to target. This is generally a good time to take a break from the thread if you're not a mafia.


Now comes the strategy/gameplay tips:
1) Make posts! (IF you're alive and it's day and they're in thread and game-relevant) Mafia runs on posts, posting is the entire game. Ideally you should be on the thread for at least 10 minutes each (real-life) day but just one post every 48 hours works too! Do try to keep them relevant to the game. For example (read this one, i define what a read is kind of)
does this post:
ostriches88 wrote:
I am eating a sandwich & playing league of dominion stars
tell you anything about the poster's alignment? No, not really. Instead you should be focusing on making reads. "reads" is the shorthand for an argument about another player. e.g.
WisteriaV wrote:
ad333 is a wolf for making a really long post, because nobody would read all of that and they know it!
(Note this isn't a particularly good read).


2) Read every post at least once.
This doesn't mean to make a read on every post, but you should read them all. It 's a better way to make more accurate reads and avoids redundancy example of players not reading
WisteriaV wrote:
I think ad333 is a mafia for making more than 10 posts
ad333 wrote:
Wow it sure is a good thing nobody thinks I'm mafia, especially you WisteriaV! That's why I think you're town
ostriches88 wrote:
Hey wisteria what's your read on ad333?
WisteriaV wrote:
ToT


3) Try to be in the thread at the same time as other people
(i.e. making posts at the same time as other people are posting). It's both more efficient and can be helpful for making reads when you're talking to somebody in "real time"
Examples
First let's look at what the opposite can look like
ad333 wrote:
Hey WisteriaV do you want to vote ostriches88
8 hours later
WisteriaV wrote:
Not particularly, he's been giving a lot of good reads on players like fdas
10 hours later
ad333 wrote:
What do you mean his read on fdas sucks; fdas does that in every game!
6 hours later
WisteriaV wrote:
Hmmm... I'll consider it. votes ostriches88
Besides taking 24 hours for something that doesn't really need that long, there's less pressure & people both have more time to craft their posts but also get across less information.
Compare that to a real-timing example:
ad333 wrote:
Hey ostriches why do you townread fdas? he does that every game
ostriches88 wrote:
Yeah but the way he does it is different, he posts twice as much when he's town
ad333 wrote:
Ok but in 250 he clearly had other motivation behind that; you pointed that out
ostriches88 wrote:
Ok i forgot but he's just town!!
ad333 wrote:
Hmm that seems like TMI


4) Watch out for classic mafia tells
The most fundamental tells could be considered TMI and awkward tone. First let's talk about awkward tone. If anybody's familiar with IRL mafia that's generally how that game is played. Players who are mafia tend to act weird because they are trying to fit in too much, and this does come across in posts too.

TMI is the general concept that mafia players know which reads are correct and which aren't which can lead to things like making reads on town players for little reason. In trying to counter this (or for other reasons) players sometimes don't take a stance on a player example
ad333 wrote:
Well I don't know... WisteriaV could be a wolf but she could also be town
I mean that is technically true but it doesn't tell you anything.


5) It's also good to find other players who are town.
If you're confident somebody else is town, congratulations you're closer to winning. If everybody can agree that 5 players (including those kiIIed at night but not at day) are town that normally lets you just kiII everybody else. But beyond that you can bounce ideas off the other person without fear that they're lying to you! Win/win.

6) Quote posts pleasee
You don;t want to be this person:
ad333 wrote:
Hey remember that time that ostriches was talking about why they think fdas is town? I think it's wolfy
Instead be this person:
ad333 wrote:
ostriches88 wrote:
fdas is town for being a great mod!
I think this is wolfy
(This one is mostly for my sanity but it makes your arguments better too!)

That's probably good enough (especially if somebody else walled but I will leave you with Secret tip for day 3/4
No seriously don't look this is a time capsule
If an experienced player who you expect to die at night has survived and you don't have a good reason for that (even if you do) you should make very sure they're town. Mafia can't die at night. Be careful because this line of reasoning can be used to mindlessly vote out such a player who's town especially if mafia is trying to push towards that goal.


Now for some FAQ*
Why don't we just hang nobody day 1?
I can't really phrase this better than an older player so...
aroodman wrote:
Often, on the first day, newbies will say things like “We shouldn’t hang today because we have no information”. The problem is that if we don’t hang, the next day will be the exact same but with one town player kiIIed by the mafia. Night kiIIs are guaranteed to hit town. Hangs aren’t. That’s why we need to hang as many times as possible.

But we don't have any information!
That's where reads come in. The way that other players post is the only real tell we have as to their alignment, this is how the game is played for the most part. This is why it's important to post, because if you have more posts you can be read better (and are reading other people more hopefully

Can I post when I'm dead?
No!!

How do I read more experienced players
I mean really just try whatever. We might object if it's leading you too far down the wrong path, but otherwise it can't really hurt. & If you want you can ask us if a read works on us and we probably won't lie. Truth be told I'm not that great at reading the other two players (don't tell them)

I have a feeling about another players alignment but cant explain it
This is not ideal, but you should post it anyways. Try to include the posts that gave you the feeling and explain it as best you can but don't worry. Maybe somebody else can figure it out, or it can be useful as a starting point for other things.

Im being attacked in the thread & cant defend myself
Yeah this is often an issue. Some solutions include: Attacking the specific arguments against you, Asking somebody else who thinks you're town to defend you, Just ignoring it and posting relatively normal anyways.

Can I say i'm mafia as a joke
I don't personally approve of it but sure go ahead, as either alignment, any role!

Are other posts going to be this long
Hopefully not but no guarantees also smh they're in hide tags it feels shorter that way

*Frequency of asked questions not guaranteed

Notes: i) examples are not really indicative of how the players type or like anything true
ii) I did write this post after getting my Role PM (but before gamestart)

Ideally you should be on the thread for at least 10 minutes each (real-life) day but just one post every 48 hours works too! Do try to keep them relevant to the game. [hide=For example (read this one, i define what a read is kind of)] does this post:
[quote=ostriches88]I am eating a sandwich & playing league of dominion stars[/quote] tell you anything about the poster's alignment? No, not really. Instead you should be focusing on making [b]reads[/b]. "reads" is the shorthand for an argument about another player. e.g.
[quote=WisteriaV]ad333 is a wolf for making a really long post, because nobody would read all of that and they know it![/quote] (Note this isn't a particularly good read).[/hide]

2) Read every post at least once.
This doesn't mean to make a read on every post, but you should read them all. It 's a better way to make more accurate reads and avoids redundancy [hide=example of players not reading][quote=WisteriaV]I think ad333 is a mafia for making more than 10 posts[/quote] [quote=ad333]Wow it sure is a good thing nobody thinks I'm mafia, especially you WisteriaV! That's why I think you're town[/quote] [quote=ostriches88]Hey wisteria what's your read on ad333?[/quote] [quote=WisteriaV]ToT[/quote][/hide]

3) Try to be in the thread at the same time as other people
(i.e. making posts at the same time as other people are posting). It's both more efficient and can be helpful for making reads when you're talking to somebody in "real time"
[hide=Examples]First let's look at what the opposite can look like
[quote=ad333]Hey WisteriaV do you want to vote ostriches88[/quote]
[i]8 hours later[/i]
[quote=WisteriaV]Not particularly, he's been giving a lot of good reads on players like fdas[/quote]
[i]10 hours later[/i]
[quote=ad333]What do you mean his read on fdas sucks; fdas does that in every game![/quote]
[i]6 hours later[/i]
[quote=WisteriaV]Hmmm... I'll consider it. [i]votes ostriches88[/i][/quote]
Besides taking 24 hours for something that doesn't really need that long, there's less pressure & people both have more time to craft their posts but also get across less information. 
Compare that to a real-timing example:
[quote=ad333]Hey ostriches why do you townread fdas? he does that every game[/quote]
[quote=ostriches88]Yeah but the way he does it is different, he posts twice as much when he's town[/quote]
[quote=ad333]Ok but in 250 he clearly had other motivation behind that; you pointed that out[/quote]
[quote=ostriches88]Ok i forgot but he's just town!![/quote]
[quote=ad333]Hmm that seems like TMI[/quote][/hide]

4) Watch out for classic mafia tells
The most [i]fundamental[/i] tells could be considered TMI and awkward tone. First let's talk about awkward tone. If anybody's familiar with IRL mafia that's generally how that game is played. Players who are mafia tend to act weird because they are trying to fit in too much, and this does come across in posts too. 

TMI is the general concept that mafia players know which reads are correct and which aren't which can lead to things like making reads on town players for little reason. In trying to counter this (or for other reasons) players sometimes don't take a stance on a player [hide=example][quote=ad333]Well I don't know... WisteriaV could be a wolf but she could also be town[/quote] I mean that is technically true but it doesn't tell you anything.[/hide]

5) It's also good to find other players who are town.
If you're confident somebody else is town, congratulations you're closer to winning. If everybody can agree that 5 players (including those kiIIed at night but not at day) are town that normally lets you just kiII everybody else. But beyond that you can bounce ideas off the other person without fear that they're lying to you! Win/win.

6) Quote posts pleasee
You don;t want to be this person:
[quote=ad333]Hey remember that time that ostriches was talking about why they think fdas is town? I think it's wolfy[/quote] Instead be this person:
[quote=ad333][quote=ostriches88]fdas is town for being a great mod![/quote]I think this is wolfy[/quote]
(This one is mostly for my sanity but it makes your arguments better too!)

That's probably good enough (especially if somebody else walled but I will leave you with [hide=Secret tip for day 3/4][hide=No seriously don't look this is a time capsule]If an experienced player who you expect to die at night has survived and you don't have a good reason for that (even if you do) you should make very sure they're town. Mafia can't die at night. Be careful because this line of reasoning can be used to mindlessly vote out such a player who's town especially if mafia is trying to push towards that goal.[/hide][/hide]

Now for some FAQ* 
[hide=Why don't we just hang nobody day 1?]I can't really phrase this better than an older player so... [quote=aroodman]Often, on the first day, newbies will say things like “We shouldn’t hang today because we have no information”. The problem is that if we don’t hang, the next day will be the exact same but with one town player kiIIed by the mafia. Night kiIIs are guaranteed to hit town. Hangs aren’t. That’s why we need to hang as many times as possible.[/quote][/hide]
[hide=But we don't have any information!]That's where reads come in. The way that other players post is the only real tell we have as to their alignment, this is how the game is played for the most part. This is why it's important to post, because if you have more posts you can be read better (and are reading other people more hopefully[/hide]
[hide=Can I post when I'm dead?]No!![/hide]
[hide=How do I read more experienced players]I mean really just try whatever. We might object if it's leading you too far down the wrong path, but otherwise it can't really hurt. & If you want you can ask us if a read works on us and we probably won't lie. Truth be told I'm not that great at reading the other two players (don't tell them)[/hide]
[hide=I have a feeling about another player's alignment but can't explain it]This is not ideal, but you should post it anyways. Try to include the posts that gave you the feeling and explain it as best you can  but don't worry. Maybe somebody else can figure it out, or it can be useful as a starting point for other things.[/hide]
[hide=I'm being attacked in the thread & can't defend myself]Yeah this is often an issue. Some solutions include: Attacking the specific arguments against you, Asking somebody else who thinks you're town to defend you, Just ignoring it and posting relatively normal anyways.[/hide]
[hide=Can I say i'm mafia as a joke]I don't personally approve of it but sure go ahead, as either alignment, any role![/hide]
[hide=Are other posts going to be this long]Hopefully not but no guarantees also smh they're in hide tags it feels shorter that way[/hide]
*Frequency of asked questions not guaranteed

Notes: i) examples are not really indicative of how the players type or like anything true
ii) I did write this post after getting my Role PM (but before gamestart)[/quote]

post_rendered:
Rules wrote:
  • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no player has been hanged, the amount of votes required for a Hang or a No Hang will be reduced by 1. The vote threshold will reduce by 1 vote every 24 hours after this. For example, if there are 8 players alive in the game, 5 votes will be required to hang someone and 4 votes are required for a no hang. Once the deadline passes, 4 votes will be sufficient to hang someone and 3 votes are sufficient for a no hang. 24 hours after the deadline, 3 votes will be sufficient to hang someone and 2 votes are sufficient for a no hang.
  • 2.10: If the vote threshold is decreased and two or more options have both reached the threshold required, then the game will enter tiebreak. During tiebreak, votes can continue to be changed. If at any point in time, only one option has enough votes to meet the threshold required for the option to be enacted, day will immediately end. Or, if any option gains votes and now exceeds the vote threshold by an amount of votes strictly greater than the amount of votes any other option exceeds their vote threshold by, that option will win and day will immediately end. If 24 hours have passed since tiebreak has begun, and the votes are still deadlocked, there will be no hang.
  • 2.11: After an option has won the vote, it will become twilight and players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.

the way setups are balanced, a certain amount of town's power to win is put into prs doing their night actions against the mafia

made by the GOAT ad333:
ad333 wrote:
Ok hi i made a wallpost for the newbies this morning because i was bored & also it's fun I'm sure this will be the one that teaches everybody everything

So Mafia was invented in 1987 by Russian professor D--
(just kidding)

Firstly, hi welcome nice to meet you all!! I'm ad333 & I'm one of the three experienced players in this newbie game, along with WisteriaV and ostriches88. We're here to help you learn how to play the game, so if you have any questions please direct them at us. This includes any terms we use that you're unfamiliar with (I'm particularly guilty of this). But for a start, there's this post!

Second, if you are not a Vanilla Townie, for the purposes of your posts here, yes you are. The following sections are obviously most optimized for players who are not a Vanilla Townie but I'd advise you give them a quick read just if you are so that you're up to date with the strategies. Also I know everybody read all the rules, including the setup but Here's how the setup works
There are 6 possible setups, and one of them is randomly chosen. Nobody knows which one was chosen, but some players have a clue. If the mafia have 2 goons, they know there is either a cop, a jailkeeper, or a tracker and 1-shot bulletproof townie, and similar if they have a roleblocker instead. And each town power role appears in 2 setups, so they also have a clue. The mystery of which setup we're in will change how mafia plays because they don't know what roles there are and have to counterplay all or none of them.
Otherwise if you're a Power Role
(Jailkeeper, Cop, Doctor, Tracker, 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie), mafia will either kiII, roleblock, or neither (if you're 1-Shot Bulletproof) you. The times when you should reveal your role are 1) If you are about to be voted out 2) If you have information that means somebody is mafia. 3) If you have information that means somebody else who is about to be voted out is town. 4) If the game reaches day 3/day 4. Hopefully one of us will be alive to explain why, but briefly this is the stage of the game where nights are scarce and the extra information is helpful. Now for specific Power Role strategies
First of all you can do something different depending on context or if you really disagree, but try to keep these in mind

1. Jailkeeper This is going to be up to your discretion largely, because you both roleblock and protect somebody. When both mafia are alive, you have less chance of targeting the specific mafia member who used the action. This also means if both of them are alive and you target somebody, but somebody dies at night this doesn't confirm them as town.
2. Cop: Again there's a number of options, but you should be trying to target people who are likely to survive to the end. If somebody is acting like a member of the mafia and will probably die the next day, it's probably not the greatest idea to check them. If they are a member of the town, you will have to claim your role and will (in most cases)not be able to use your role again.

You should also find a way to hide your results in your posts, so that in case you die at night without having claimed, we can use the fact that you are a cop to figure out who you checked during the night. This is probably easiest to do by just moving the player up in your reads (if they're town, if they're mafia you should probably just tell everybody that you are the cop and that player is mafia).

3. Doctor: Target whoever you think is going to die. This is normally either a player who is experienced (so that they stop posting in the thread and don't have the chance to figure out who the mafia team is), a player who is towny (because they won't ever get hanged), or a Power Role (normally only one that has revealed themselves). Since mafia normally don't even know if there is a doctor you shouldn't worry too much about trying to outsmart them.

4. Tracker: Ok I'm not going to lie this is one of the weaker roles, but it still can do great things. The first thing to know is the difference between a "No result" and "no visit" answer. The first one means you were roleblocked, the second means your target didn't visit anybody.

If you do get a result that a player visited someone, if they are the player who died, that is the mafia. If not, that's the doctor. If you get a "no visit" result, they could be the mafia whose partner did the kiII instead. This means you should try to target the player who is most likely to be mafia doing a nightkiII. This is subject to the mafia trying to outsmart you so be careful. Once only one mafia is alive you become a lot more powerful, congratulations! You are now basically a cop

5. 1-Shot BulletProof townie. Some people consider this a boring role, I don't. Your goal is to get mafia to try to kiII you, normally by being very towny, or just by surviving until mafia have fewer choices. That is pretty much it.
If you're mafia
First off, we are enemies, but it's my job to help you too (unless the mafia is exactly WisteriaV/ostriches88 in which case I hate you both). It's it's not very fun for anybody if you tell everyone else that you are mafia. That's about it. KiII the players who
1) Are most likely to successfully vote you out
2) Are least likely to get voted out
and/or
3) Have claimed a Power Role
As for how to post... You'll normally have to figure that out but try to post like you are town. As a mafia you have TMI, more on this later, but it makes it hard for you to accurately pretend to be mafia.

Also it might be worth figuring out what to do if your partner is under pressure. Do you try to save them (<- preferred) or vote them out as well to seem extra towny.
Ok I know I just spent a lot of time talking about power Roles but really, these shouldn't matter until at least day 2 and it's best not to talk about them because that's a easy way for mafia to figure out what your role is.

Third, let's talk about the structure of the game. I reccomend you read this just to clear up any questions about this sort of stuff Game structure
There are 7 town members and 2 mafia members. I'll do my best to call them mafia throughout this post & the game but mafia are also known as wolves or scum. The mafia want to kiII everyone and the town want to stop that by voting out the mafia. The town's strength is their numbers, the mafia's strength is knowing each other (and their night kiII).

The game alternates between day and night phases, starting with day 1 right now. During the day, players make posts, and then based on those posts they vote somebody out. It's important not to edit or delete posts or to talk about the game even to people not in the game because it can create information imbalances that shouldn't be there. Some examples
In more malicious cases a player can make a post while one other player is in the thread, claiming a role, for example, and then edit it once they've seen it. This is bad and is as such against the rules. Even if you talk to someone outside of the game, there can be issues such as that player talking to somebody else in the game, or them giving you ideas. Note that this is not an invitation to break the rules or to challenge them (in thread, you can go bug fdas if you want); it's just the first things that come to mind as examples for why the exist. Just don't.
Make a new post in the thread if you want to edit, and if you want to talk about the game outside of the thread... either talk in thread or in your Role PM, or wait for the game to end. The wolves have a private chat where they can talk to each other during the day and the night.

During the night, nobody can post in the thread. The mafia choose somebody to kiII, and the power roles choose somebody to target. This is generally a good time to take a break from the thread if you're not a mafia.


Now comes the strategy/gameplay tips:
1) Make posts! (IF you're alive and it's day and they're in thread and game-relevant) Mafia runs on posts, posting is the entire game. Ideally you should be on the thread for at least 10 minutes each (real-life) day but just one post every 48 hours works too! Do try to keep them relevant to the game. For example (read this one, i define what a read is kind of)
does this post:
ostriches88 wrote:
I am eating a sandwich & playing league of dominion stars
tell you anything about the poster's alignment? No, not really. Instead you should be focusing on making reads. "reads" is the shorthand for an argument about another player. e.g.
WisteriaV wrote:
ad333 is a wolf for making a really long post, because nobody would read all of that and they know it!
(Note this isn't a particularly good read).


2) Read every post at least once.
This doesn't mean to make a read on every post, but you should read them all. It 's a better way to make more accurate reads and avoids redundancy example of players not reading
WisteriaV wrote:
I think ad333 is a mafia for making more than 10 posts
ad333 wrote:
Wow it sure is a good thing nobody thinks I'm mafia, especially you WisteriaV! That's why I think you're town
ostriches88 wrote:
Hey wisteria what's your read on ad333?
WisteriaV wrote:
ToT


3) Try to be in the thread at the same time as other people
(i.e. making posts at the same time as other people are posting). It's both more efficient and can be helpful for making reads when you're talking to somebody in "real time"
Examples
First let's look at what the opposite can look like
ad333 wrote:
Hey WisteriaV do you want to vote ostriches88
8 hours later
WisteriaV wrote:
Not particularly, he's been giving a lot of good reads on players like fdas
10 hours later
ad333 wrote:
What do you mean his read on fdas sucks; fdas does that in every game!
6 hours later
WisteriaV wrote:
Hmmm... I'll consider it. votes ostriches88
Besides taking 24 hours for something that doesn't really need that long, there's less pressure & people both have more time to craft their posts but also get across less information.
Compare that to a real-timing example:
ad333 wrote:
Hey ostriches why do you townread fdas? he does that every game
ostriches88 wrote:
Yeah but the way he does it is different, he posts twice as much when he's town
ad333 wrote:
Ok but in 250 he clearly had other motivation behind that; you pointed that out
ostriches88 wrote:
Ok i forgot but he's just town!!
ad333 wrote:
Hmm that seems like TMI


4) Watch out for classic mafia tells
The most fundamental tells could be considered TMI and awkward tone. First let's talk about awkward tone. If anybody's familiar with IRL mafia that's generally how that game is played. Players who are mafia tend to act weird because they are trying to fit in too much, and this does come across in posts too.

TMI is the general concept that mafia players know which reads are correct and which aren't which can lead to things like making reads on town players for little reason. In trying to counter this (or for other reasons) players sometimes don't take a stance on a player example
ad333 wrote:
Well I don't know... WisteriaV could be a wolf but she could also be town
I mean that is technically true but it doesn't tell you anything.


5) It's also good to find other players who are town.
If you're confident somebody else is town, congratulations you're closer to winning. If everybody can agree that 5 players (including those kiIIed at night but not at day) are town that normally lets you just kiII everybody else. But beyond that you can bounce ideas off the other person without fear that they're lying to you! Win/win.

6) Quote posts pleasee
You don;t want to be this person:
ad333 wrote:
Hey remember that time that ostriches was talking about why they think fdas is town? I think it's wolfy
Instead be this person:
ad333 wrote:
ostriches88 wrote:
fdas is town for being a great mod!
I think this is wolfy
(This one is mostly for my sanity but it makes your arguments better too!)

That's probably good enough (especially if somebody else walled but I will leave you with Secret tip for day 3/4
No seriously don't look this is a time capsule
If an experienced player who you expect to die at night has survived and you don't have a good reason for that (even if you do) you should make very sure they're town. Mafia can't die at night. Be careful because this line of reasoning can be used to mindlessly vote out such a player who's town especially if mafia is trying to push towards that goal.


Now for some FAQ*
Why don't we just hang nobody day 1?
I can't really phrase this better than an older player so...
aroodman wrote:
Often, on the first day, newbies will say things like “We shouldn’t hang today because we have no information”. The problem is that if we don’t hang, the next day will be the exact same but with one town player kiIIed by the mafia. Night kiIIs are guaranteed to hit town. Hangs aren’t. That’s why we need to hang as many times as possible.

But we don't have any information!
That's where reads come in. The way that other players post is the only real tell we have as to their alignment, this is how the game is played for the most part. This is why it's important to post, because if you have more posts you can be read better (and are reading other people more hopefully

Can I post when I'm dead?
No!!

How do I read more experienced players
I mean really just try whatever. We might object if it's leading you too far down the wrong path, but otherwise it can't really hurt. & If you want you can ask us if a read works on us and we probably won't lie. Truth be told I'm not that great at reading the other two players (don't tell them)

I have a feeling about another players alignment but cant explain it
This is not ideal, but you should post it anyways. Try to include the posts that gave you the feeling and explain it as best you can but don't worry. Maybe somebody else can figure it out, or it can be useful as a starting point for other things.

Im being attacked in the thread & cant defend myself
Yeah this is often an issue. Some solutions include: Attacking the specific arguments against you, Asking somebody else who thinks you're town to defend you, Just ignoring it and posting relatively normal anyways.

Can I say i'm mafia as a joke
I don't personally approve of it but sure go ahead, as either alignment, any role!

Are other posts going to be this long
Hopefully not but no guarantees also smh they're in hide tags it feels shorter that way

*Frequency of asked questions not guaranteed

Notes: i) examples are not really indicative of how the players type or like anything true
ii) I did write this post after getting my Role PM (but before gamestart)

made by the GOAT ad333:

[quote=ad333]Ok hi i made a wallpost for the newbies this morning because i was bored & also it's fun I'm sure this will be the one that teaches everybody everything

So Mafia was invented in 1987 by Russian professor D--
(just kidding)

Firstly, hi welcome nice to meet you all!! I'm ad333 & I'm one of the three experienced players in this newbie game, along with WisteriaV and ostriches88. We're here to help you learn how to play the game, so if you have any questions please direct them at us. This includes any terms we use that you're unfamiliar with (I'm particularly guilty of this). But for a start, there's this post!

Second, if you are not a Vanilla Townie, for the purposes of your posts here, yes you are. The following sections are obviously most optimized for players who are not a Vanilla Townie but I'd advise you give them a quick read just if you are so that you're up to date with the strategies. Also I know everybody read all the rules, including the setup but [hide=Here's how the setup works]There are 6 possible setups, and one of them is randomly chosen. Nobody knows which one was chosen, but some players have a clue. If the mafia have 2 goons, they know there is either a cop, a jailkeeper, or a tracker and 1-shot bulletproof townie, and similar if they have a roleblocker instead. And each town power role appears in 2 setups, so they also have a clue. The mystery of which setup we're in will change how mafia plays because they don't know what roles there are and have to counterplay all or none of them.[/hide][hide=Otherwise if you're a Power Role] (Jailkeeper, Cop, Doctor, Tracker, 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie), mafia will either kiII, roleblock, or neither (if you're 1-Shot Bulletproof) you. The times when you should reveal your role are 1) If you are about to be voted out 2) If you have information that means somebody is mafia. 3) If you have information that means somebody else who is about to be voted out is town. 4) If the game reaches day 3/day 4. Hopefully one of us will be alive to explain why, but briefly this is the stage of the game where nights are scarce and the extra information is helpful. [hide=Now for specific Power Role strategies]First of all you can do something different depending on context or if you really disagree, but try to keep these in mind

1. Jailkeeper This is going to be up to your discretion largely, because you both roleblock and protect somebody. When both mafia are alive, you have less chance of targeting [i]the specific mafia member who used the action[/i]. This also means if both of them are alive and you target somebody, but somebody dies at night this doesn't confirm them as town.
2. Cop: Again there's a number of options, but you should be trying to target people [i]who are likely to survive to the end[/i]. If somebody is acting like a member of the mafia and will probably die the next day, it's probably not the greatest idea to check them. If they are a member of the town, you will have to claim your role and will (in most cases)not be able to use your role again. 

You should also find a way to hide your results in your posts, so that in case you die at night without having claimed, we can use the fact that you are a cop to figure out who you checked during the night. This is probably easiest to do by just moving the player up in your reads (if they're town, if they're mafia you should probably just tell everybody that you are the cop and that player is mafia).

3. Doctor: Target whoever you think is going to die. This is normally either  a player who is experienced (so that they stop posting in the thread and don't have the chance to figure out who the mafia team is), a player who is towny (because they won't ever get hanged), or a Power Role (normally only one that has revealed themselves). Since mafia normally don't even know if there is a doctor you shouldn't worry too much about trying to outsmart them.

4. Tracker: Ok I'm not going to lie this is one of the weaker roles, but it still can do great things. The first thing to know is the difference between a "No result" and "no visit" answer. The first one means you were roleblocked, the second means your target didn't visit anybody. 

If you do get a result that a player visited someone, if they are the player who died, that is the mafia. If not, that's the doctor. If you get a "no visit" result, they could be the mafia whose partner did the kiII instead. This means you should try to target [i]the player who is most likely to be mafia doing a nightkiII[/i]. This is subject to the mafia trying to outsmart you so be careful. Once only one mafia is alive you become a lot more powerful, congratulations! You are now basically a cop

5. 1-Shot BulletProof townie. Some people consider this a boring role, I don't. Your goal is to [i]get mafia to try to kiII you[/i], normally by being very towny, or just by surviving until mafia have fewer choices. That is pretty much it. 
[/hide][/hide][hide=If you're mafia]First off, we are enemies, but it's my job to help you too (unless the mafia is exactly WisteriaV/ostriches88 in which case I hate you both). It's it's not very fun for anybody if you tell everyone else that you are mafia. That's about it. KiII the players who 
1) Are most likely to successfully vote you out
2) Are least likely to get voted out
and/or
3) Have claimed a Power Role
As for how to post... You'll normally have to figure that out but try to post like you are town. As a mafia you have [b]TMI[/b], more on this later, but it makes it hard for you to accurately pretend to be mafia.

Also it might be worth figuring out what to do if your partner is under pressure. Do you try to save them (<- preferred) or vote them out as well to seem extra towny.[/hide] Ok I know I just spent a lot of time talking about power Roles but really, these shouldn't matter until [i]at least[/i] day 2 and it's best not to talk about them because that's a easy way for mafia to figure out what your role is.

Third, let's talk about the structure of the game. I reccomend you read this just to clear up any questions about this sort of stuff [hide=Game structure]There are 7 town members and 2 mafia members. I'll do my best to call them mafia throughout this post & the game but mafia are also known as wolves or scum. The mafia want to kiII everyone and the town want to stop that by voting out the mafia. The town's strength is their numbers, the mafia's strength is knowing each other (and their night kiII).

The game alternates between day and night phases, starting with day 1 right now. During the day, players make posts, and then based on those posts they vote somebody out. It's important not to edit or delete posts or to talk about the game even to people not in the game because it can create information imbalances that shouldn't be there. [hide=Some examples]In more malicious cases a player can make a post while one other player is in the thread, claiming a role, for example, and then edit it once they've seen it. This is bad and is as such against the rules. Even if you talk to someone outside of the game, there can be issues such as that player talking to somebody else in the game, or them giving you ideas. Note that this is not an invitation to break the rules or to challenge them (in thread, you can go bug fdas if you want); it's just the first things that come to mind as examples for why the exist. Just don't.[/hide] Make a new post in the thread if you want to edit, and if you want to talk about the game outside of the thread... either talk in thread or in your Role PM, or wait for the game to end. The wolves have a private chat where they can talk to each other during the day and the night.

During the night, nobody can post in the thread. The mafia choose somebody to kiII, and the power roles choose somebody to target. This is generally a good time to take a break from the thread if you're not a mafia.[/hide]

Now comes the strategy/gameplay tips:
1) Make posts! (IF you're alive and it's day and they're in thread and game-relevant) Mafia runs on posts, posting is the entire game. Ideally you should be on the thread for at least 10 minutes each (real-life) day but just one post every 48 hours works too! Do try to keep them relevant to the game. [hide=For example (read this one, i define what a read is kind of)] does this post:
[quote=ostriches88]I am eating a sandwich & playing league of dominion stars[/quote] tell you anything about the poster's alignment? No, not really. Instead you should be focusing on making [b]reads[/b]. "reads" is the shorthand for an argument about another player. e.g.
[quote=WisteriaV]ad333 is a wolf for making a really long post, because nobody would read all of that and they know it![/quote] (Note this isn't a particularly good read).[/hide]

2) Read every post at least once.
This doesn't mean to make a read on every post, but you should read them all. It 's a better way to make more accurate reads and avoids redundancy [hide=example of players not reading][quote=WisteriaV]I think ad333 is a mafia for making more than 10 posts[/quote] [quote=ad333]Wow it sure is a good thing nobody thinks I'm mafia, especially you WisteriaV! That's why I think you're town[/quote] [quote=ostriches88]Hey wisteria what's your read on ad333?[/quote] [quote=WisteriaV]ToT[/quote][/hide]

3) Try to be in the thread at the same time as other people
(i.e. making posts at the same time as other people are posting). It's both more efficient and can be helpful for making reads when you're talking to somebody in "real time"
[hide=Examples]First let's look at what the opposite can look like
[quote=ad333]Hey WisteriaV do you want to vote ostriches88[/quote]
[i]8 hours later[/i]
[quote=WisteriaV]Not particularly, he's been giving a lot of good reads on players like fdas[/quote]
[i]10 hours later[/i]
[quote=ad333]What do you mean his read on fdas sucks; fdas does that in every game![/quote]
[i]6 hours later[/i]
[quote=WisteriaV]Hmmm... I'll consider it. [i]votes ostriches88[/i][/quote]
Besides taking 24 hours for something that doesn't really need that long, there's less pressure & people both have more time to craft their posts but also get across less information. 
Compare that to a real-timing example:
[quote=ad333]Hey ostriches why do you townread fdas? he does that every game[/quote]
[quote=ostriches88]Yeah but the way he does it is different, he posts twice as much when he's town[/quote]
[quote=ad333]Ok but in 250 he clearly had other motivation behind that; you pointed that out[/quote]
[quote=ostriches88]Ok i forgot but he's just town!![/quote]
[quote=ad333]Hmm that seems like TMI[/quote][/hide]

4) Watch out for classic mafia tells
The most [i]fundamental[/i] tells could be considered TMI and awkward tone. First let's talk about awkward tone. If anybody's familiar with IRL mafia that's generally how that game is played. Players who are mafia tend to act weird because they are trying to fit in too much, and this does come across in posts too. 

TMI is the general concept that mafia players know which reads are correct and which aren't which can lead to things like making reads on town players for little reason. In trying to counter this (or for other reasons) players sometimes don't take a stance on a player [hide=example][quote=ad333]Well I don't know... WisteriaV could be a wolf but she could also be town[/quote] I mean that is technically true but it doesn't tell you anything.[/hide]

5) It's also good to find other players who are town.
If you're confident somebody else is town, congratulations you're closer to winning. If everybody can agree that 5 players (including those kiIIed at night but not at day) are town that normally lets you just kiII everybody else. But beyond that you can bounce ideas off the other person without fear that they're lying to you! Win/win.

6) Quote posts pleasee
You don;t want to be this person:
[quote=ad333]Hey remember that time that ostriches was talking about why they think fdas is town? I think it's wolfy[/quote] Instead be this person:
[quote=ad333][quote=ostriches88]fdas is town for being a great mod![/quote]I think this is wolfy[/quote]
(This one is mostly for my sanity but it makes your arguments better too!)

That's probably good enough (especially if somebody else walled but I will leave you with [hide=Secret tip for day 3/4][hide=No seriously don't look this is a time capsule]If an experienced player who you expect to die at night has survived and you don't have a good reason for that (even if you do) you should make very sure they're town. Mafia can't die at night. Be careful because this line of reasoning can be used to mindlessly vote out such a player who's town especially if mafia is trying to push towards that goal.[/hide][/hide]

Now for some FAQ* 
[hide=Why don't we just hang nobody day 1?]I can't really phrase this better than an older player so... [quote=aroodman]Often, on the first day, newbies will say things like “We shouldn’t hang today because we have no information”. The problem is that if we don’t hang, the next day will be the exact same but with one town player kiIIed by the mafia. Night kiIIs are guaranteed to hit town. Hangs aren’t. That’s why we need to hang as many times as possible.[/quote][/hide]
[hide=But we don't have any information!]That's where reads come in. The way that other players post is the only real tell we have as to their alignment, this is how the game is played for the most part. This is why it's important to post, because if you have more posts you can be read better (and are reading other people more hopefully[/hide]
[hide=Can I post when I'm dead?]No!![/hide]
[hide=How do I read more experienced players]I mean really just try whatever. We might object if it's leading you too far down the wrong path, but otherwise it can't really hurt. & If you want you can ask us if a read works on us and we probably won't lie. Truth be told I'm not that great at reading the other two players (don't tell them)[/hide]
[hide=I have a feeling about another player's alignment but can't explain it]This is not ideal, but you should post it anyways. Try to include the posts that gave you the feeling and explain it as best you can  but don't worry. Maybe somebody else can figure it out, or it can be useful as a starting point for other things.[/hide]
[hide=I'm being attacked in the thread & can't defend myself]Yeah this is often an issue. Some solutions include: Attacking the specific arguments against you, Asking somebody else who thinks you're town to defend you, Just ignoring it and posting relatively normal anyways.[/hide]
[hide=Can I say i'm mafia as a joke]I don't personally approve of it but sure go ahead, as either alignment, any role![/hide]
[hide=Are other posts going to be this long]Hopefully not but no guarantees also smh they're in hide tags it feels shorter that way[/hide]
*Frequency of asked questions not guaranteed

Notes: i) examples are not really indicative of how the players type or like anything true
ii) I did write this post after getting my Role PM (but before gamestart)[/quote][/quote]

post_rendered:
hockey - uncced 1sbp

mj - i think the key indicator about mj is the reads and pushes he makes. around midway to late d1 i mentioned that i had a "stupid reason" for tr'ing mj, which was that mj was pretty combative of my takes instead of pocketing me. given that we were the two leading voices of the thread at the time, i think him not sucking up to me and reinforcing his own independent worldview is actually very towny, since i was expecting him to try and agree with my reads to pocket and influence me that way if he was wolf. additionally i think his tunnel on strife is fairly towny; despite my various attempts, he was extremely convicted on the read and i feel like he would be way more likely to just be a townie with extreme confbias than a scum intentionally limiting his own worldview. plus his lack of energy coming into d2 instead of trying to influence the next hang does not seem like what he would do as scum
there are other things such as one of his offhand comments saying that he would have had way more fun during eod1 as scum that also made me independently tr him, and i think im comfortable keeping him at a tr today given the reasons i have listed above

fdas - i've agreed with a bunch of his takes regarding slots in relation to the gamestate, which might count for something, but despite his criticism of eod i am a little concerned with his strife vote. his reason for voting that way was to sheep mj, and in the context of a slow moving eod where strife got mishanged im slightly suspicious of his vote and commitment to the strife wagon. i feel like its hard for me to judge fdas otherwise because of a lack of posts and overall content and i dont think the mysterious aura is helping a lot with that fact, but i also believe there are much more blatantly scummy slots with extreme scum equity, and hopefully i will be able to successfully evaluate fdas with more info

unicode - i am pretty greatly concerned about unicode. the reason why i asked they about his continued sb vote all the way till eod was because i wanted to see if they was doing any re-evaluation whatsoever, and their answer bothers me because it doesnt sound like there was any evolution in they sb read since very early in d1, and even after theta started posting. this kind of detachment from the gamestate especially when strife got mishanged is extremely scummy, and although they also sr lhr, i honestly do not like their casual willingness to push the wagon. im lacking the "care" of the game that they supposedly have according to their last post.

lhr - i've listed out my reasons already, but i will respond to one of fdas' points here
fdas in p#1067 wrote:

I do not think that if lhr was mafia, they would have showed up at eod asking us what to do. Surely their partner would be telling them something at least in wolfchat so that lhr isnt so ignorant of the game mechanics.

what i witnessed in 315 was actually the opposite where azure let peachfox stay clueless in order to draw their ire. i think derpclearing lhr is a massive mistake because of that, and we should simply evaluate the content of their posts, which ar eextremely scummy.

To run you through my reads on lhr:

For reasons I've previously stated, i think fdas and mj are the two towniest people in thread. hockeyfox is confirmed. that leaves three people: Euler, lhr, unicode.
Euler/lhr seems extremely unlikely due to the nature of how euler forced lhr to die here and is actively shutting down counterwagons.
That leaves unicode/euler and unicode/lhr. Because of this, unicode is the correct vote fmpov. If I'm wrong on fdas and he's wolf then sure lhr might be a wolfpartner or maybe euler or mj but I just think that if this happens to go into f5 it'll be much easier to deal with than if we have a big question mark of a hang. lhr has done nothing in thread and so we have no good reason to say they'll flip town or wolf here. And banking on them being apart of an unplausible pairing with fdas just doesn't make sense here. Hope you can see my concern.

My arguments for FDAs being town were all completely real solving with very little TMI or agenda influencing it. - hang Unicode who I suspected to be PR
- buddy with FDAs and pair us
- antipair me and Euler
This game is very reflective on my growth as a mafia player because I tried this exact same strategy of pairing myself as a wolfy player with a towny who was also being wolfread in order to try and tunnel people into both of us. Again, congrats to Euler and K12 you both played great!

If I had to say something positive about each player I think mainly
1) Count was probably the strongest townie this game imo and even though she got tunneled on FDAs in f5 she by far did the best in solving by being able to clear Unicode. I think she should have tried to get a hang on me especially since she read Unicode as pr bc then you don’t have to worry about paired allegations.
2) Hockey did a good job too and honestly they’re very hard to read in a good way. It’s very difficult for me to read you as scum or town because you have very little posting differences which is why it was hard to find you in 313. One thing you could have done better with was maybe solving more in-thread so people could understand your thoughts a bit more.
3) fdas. Very good play and reasonably got hung up on Unicode. Good job on finding Euler! There’s nothing I can really say on what he can improve this game. Obviously this isn’t an expectation and it’s kind of disappointing the game would have to go in this direction but I think you could have read Unicode as PR there if you weren’t tunneled. Obviously if someone isn’t hard softing they could just be wolf but Unicode has clear PR excitement in my eyes whereas when he’s VT or wolf he loses interest in the game easily. I think you should have tried getting him to claim earlier once you or Euler or whoever was second on that vote did it. I also made the mistake of waiting too long to clear him or asking him to do something to save his position when he couldn’t tie the vote to save his life in 312.
4) MJ. Uh idk man you did very good on day 1 even if you were wrong. I think the strategy on reading strife there was to tell how disengaged they were from the start of the game. Even beyond all of their excuses at the beginning of the game they clearly looked disinterested and the only reason for that is probably what alignment they rolled (a newbwolf won’t care who their scum partner is initially). There were a few things to deduce that they hated towning (either that they directly said it in-thread or that most newbies have wolf excitement).
5) Unicode. Honestly I think you did okay in this game. The best thing you did was being more active in this game. I think you need to get better at eod scenarios. Unlike 312, you did self pres this time, but you also need to claim and be online for EoD.
6) Sweetbird/theta. I didn’t read this slot but sweetbird you just read as wolfy this game bc you were like idk larping and joking around with mj and it made it hard to take your reads seriously because of how flippant you were with them. Like, when I flip flop my reads and make a ton of jokes it’s different. I can do that because a) it’s my town playstyle, and b) I’m basically a grown man at this point and you’re 11 years old so me acting immature is clearly secret method TM and you acting immature is a lot harder to read as “MJ breed town meta” and easier to read as “immature 11 year old gets first Wolfrand and thinks he’s in the big leagues with MJ”
7) strife. Strife also did good in this game. I think the main thing is just improving your interest in the game and trying to be better at solving.

But also take my advice with a grain of salt I replaced in late day 2 and Yknow I’m not some pro so yeah.
